visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a dynamic title sequence overlaying the interior of a Costco warehouse parking lot. A cartoon character, Ralph from Wreck-It Ralph, is edited into the left side. The environment is bright with white industrial fluorescent lighting and rows of parked cars in the background. Large, colorful text overlays the center of the frame, advertising weekly specials. (2) The scene shifts to a series of fast-paced cuts showcasing various consumer products on warehouse shelves. A person's hand intermittently enters the frame to point out or touch the packaging of items like ice cream, laundry beads, and diapers. Each product is shown in its original retail packaging, neatly stacked in large quantities on industrial metal shelving units. (3) The final segment continues the rapid-fire product showcase, moving through aisles containing food items, snacks, beverages, and household goods. The camera uses handheld movements and quick pans to highlight the specific products. High-contrast price tags and discount information are superimposed over every item, clearly visible against the backdrop of the warehouse aisles and refrigerated sections.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 individual whose hands are visible pointing at products.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Only the hands of the creator are visible, occasionally entering the frame to point at product features.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The interior of a Costco Wholesale warehouse, featuring high metal industrial shelving, refrigerated display cases, and pallets of bulk goods under bright artificial lighting.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio track opens with a high-energy male narrator speaking rapidly in Traditional Chinese, introducing the week's specials at Costco. The background music is an upbeat, high-tempo pop track with a synthesized melody and a consistent driving beat. A distinct 'pop' sound effect punctuates the start of the narration. (2) As the narrator lists each item, a sharp 'ding' sound effect triggers every time a price appears on the screen. The narrator's pitch is energetic and rising, maintaining a high level of arousal to engage the listener. The background music remains steady, providing a rhythmic bed for the rapid-fire speech. (3) The sequence continues with the narrator announcing names and discounted prices in quick succession. Each new product transition is accompanied by a subtle transition sound and another high-pitched chime. The vocal delivery is clear and professionally projected, with minimal background noise or interference, indicating a post-produced and highly polished audio layout.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Enumeration -> Continuous Product List
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To quickly inform viewers about current weekly discounts and new product arrivals at Costco, encouraging them to visit the store during the specified dates.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Detailed pricing, discounts, and brand-specific showcases indicate a promotional intent for the featured products.

high_inference.freeDescribe: High-density retail promotional video using social-native fast-cut editing and synchronized audio-visual cues to maintain high viewer engagement.
